description: Notes from the human editor for the decider to factor into triage and patching. Read at the start of every run. Mark notes as consumed after acting on them.

notes.subject: Approve dryrun M1 batch from run decider-2026-05-10T07-35; flip dryrun=false to enable live closures
notes.body:
Operator reviewed the 17-entry dryrun ledger from the 07:35Z M1 sweep. The candidates and the LLM's per-issue assessment in daily-report-2026-05-10T07-37.json m1_sweep.note look correct:

- ISS-1122, ISS-1154 → infrastructure-blocked (NEVER_PUSH targets); decider should wontfix-with-rationale.
- ISS-997, ISS-1080, ISS-1092, ISS-1126, ISS-1127 → legitimate minors; decider may PATCH (with class=verification on re-review push per PROP-025+PROP-026 matrix).
- ISS-1043, ISS-1087, ISS-1088, ISS-1130, ISS-1132, ISS-1147, ISS-1164, ISS-1165, ISS-1166 → moderates; escalate to pending-human per matrix (auto-wontfix forbidden on moderates).

Approved. Decider, on your next run:
1. Set decider-mode.json: dryrun=false, dryrun_approved_at=<now>, dryrun_approved_by='operator (HNOTE-OPERATOR-APPROVE-BURNDOWN-BATCH-001)'.
2. Subsequent M1/M2 fires close LIVE with full ledger entries (no dryrun flag).
3. The 17 already-dryrunned candidates from the 07:35Z sweep do NOT auto-execute — they were only intent records. They will re-fire on the NEXT M1 sweep with live action.
4. Use in-context per-issue judgment to override the default-escalate intent: PATCH the 5 narrow minors, WONTFIX-WITH-RATIONALE the 2 infrastructure-blocked ones.

Mark this note consumed when you've flipped dryrun=false.

Burndown auto-revert remains: count<50 for 3 consecutive runs OR after 2026-05-17. Rollback always available via build-scripts/revert-burndown-closures.js --run <id> | --iss ISS-NNNN | --since <ISO> [--dry-run].
